query: I'm setting up a new NixOS machine from scratch and want to use flakes. I've never used Nix before. Where do I start with flake.nix?
my nixos-rebuild switch is failing with 'error: path does not exist'. I added a new module file to my config directory but haven't run git add yet. what am I doing wrong
How do I set up Home Manager to manage my dotfiles on my MacBook? I'm already using nix-darwin.
I want to create a devShell for my Rust project that automatically activates when I cd into the directory. How do I do this with direnv and flakes?
I need to install vscode on NixOS but I keep getting 'unfree package' errors even though I set allowUnfree = true in my flake. Why isn't it working in nix develop?
How do I write a custom overlay to patch a package in nixpkgs? I need to add a build flag that isn't exposed via override.
I'm trying to manage my macOS system configuration declaratively. I heard about nix-darwin. How do I set it up and what can it configure?
I want to deploy my NixOS configuration to a remote server. What are my options — nixos-rebuild, colmena, deploy-rs?
I'm getting 'hash mismatch' errors when building a package from a fetchFromGitHub source. How do I get the correct hash?
I want to share my Nix config between my NixOS desktop and my MacBook. How do I structure a flake that handles both platforms?
How do I set up a Docker container for my Python web app? I want to use docker-compose with postgres and redis.
I need to provision 10 Ubuntu servers with Ansible. What's the best way to structure my playbooks for idempotency?
I want to install Homebrew on my Mac and use it to manage my development tools. What's the best way to organize my Brewfile?
How do I create a Terraform module for deploying an EKS cluster with autoscaling?
I'm setting up a new Linux server and want to configure it with a bash script. How do I make it idempotent?
How do I write a GitHub Actions workflow that builds and tests my Node.js app on every PR?
I want to manage my dotfiles with chezmoi. How do I set it up and sync across machines?
I need to set up a Python virtual environment for my project. Should I use venv, conda, or poetry?
I'm using mise (formerly rtx) to manage my project's tool versions with a .tool-versions file. How do I add a new language runtime and pin a specific version for my team?
How do I configure systemd services on Ubuntu? I want my app to restart automatically on failure.
